pi_items.metadata_value: QBlade (implied from task description and hooks)
pi_items.verified_value: QBlade - a real, open-source wind turbine simulation software by TU Berlin/Institute for Fluid Mechanics
BPM model is a real semi-empirical acoustic prediction method developed by Brooks, Pope, and Marcolini for wind turbine noise prediction
pi_items.source: https://github.com/byuflowlab/bpm-turbine-acoustics and academic literature on wind turbine acoustics

pi_items.verified_value: NREL 5MW Reference Turbine - an actual 5MW offshore wind turbine baseline model defined by NREL in February 2009

pi_items.source: Wind turbine noise measurement standards typically use distances from 150m to 1.5km; 500m is a common intermediate measurement distance used in aeroacoustic studies. Verified through ISO and IEC standards on wind turbine noise measurement.

Standard wind turbine noise measurement protocols specify microphone heights of 1.2-1.5m above ground for ground-level measurements, or on building facades at height of occupancy. 2m is a reasonable and commonly used measurement height for downwind observer positions.
NREL technical report on NREL 5MW Reference Turbine specifications confirms optimal TSR of 7.55 at peak power coefficient. Modern 3-bladed HAWTs typically operate in the 6-8 range.

pi_items.metadata_value: noise_spectrum_output.txt (text file with Frequency vs SPL)
pi_items.verified_value: QBlade supports text format (.txt) export for aeroacoustic simulation results. Spectrum data (Frequency vs SPL/dBA) is standard output format for acoustic simulations.
.wpa is the confirmed QBlade project file format
pi_items.source: https://filext.com/file-extension/WPA confirms .wpa files are QBlade wind turbine project files; verified through QBlade forums and source code

pi_items.verified_value: Unverifiable - this is a threshold set by task creator for what constitutes valid noise spectrum data
pi_items.source: Task definition only; no independent source for minimum line count in spectra

pi_items.verified_value: Unverifiable - project file size depends on simulation complexity and content
pi_items.source: Task definition only; typical QBlade project files with full aeroacoustic simulations are generally >50KB based on structure

privileged_info_summary: This task is based on real wind energy and acoustics standards. QBlade is an actual, open-source wind turbine simulation software with aeroacoustic capabilities. The BPM (Brooks-Pope-Marcolini) model is a well-established semi-empirical acoustic prediction method widely used in wind energy. The NREL 5MW Reference Turbine is a real baseline wind turbine model published by NREL with well-documented specifications. The target Tip Speed Ratio of 7-8 is realistic: the NREL 5MW optimally operates at TSR 7.55 during below-rated wind speeds. Observer position (500m downwind, 2m height) aligns with standard wind turbine noise measurement protocols. File formats are authentic: .wpa is QBlade's native project format, and .txt is a supported export format for aeroacoustic data. The task involves genuine wind energy engineering workflows: BEM simulation → Noise prediction module → Spectrum export.
